triageFlags.title: Credit Score Below Acceptable Threshold
triageFlags.description: The applicant's credit score is below the minimum threshold for approval.
triageFlags.recommendation: Decline the application unless exceptional circumstances are documented.
triageFlags.reasoning: According to the policy, a credit score below 550 is an automatic decline unless there are exceptional, documented circumstances.

triageFlags.title: DTI Ratio Close to Maximum Allowable Limit
triageFlags.description: The applicant's DTI ratio is close to the maximum allowable limit for their credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Verify all income and expense details to ensure the DTI calculation is accurate.
triageFlags.reasoning: The DTI ratio is a critical measure of affordability, and being close to the limit requires careful verification to avoid overextension of the applicant's finances.

content.reasoning: The applicant's credit score places them in a high-risk category, and while the DTI ratio is within the acceptable range, it is close to the threshold, necessitating further scrutiny to ensure affordability.
